items.title: Countdown is on! Behind the scenes access as Rozicki trains for Billam-Smith fight
items.summary: Sky Sports News reporter Andy Scott gives exclusive access to one of Ryan Rozicki's final training sessions ahead of his fight with Chris Billam-Smith and interviews his trainer Shaun George.

items.title: Why we could see less of Kane for England at the 2026 World Cup...
items.summary: England have three strikers at this World Cup, and Harry Kane's replacements have never had such an important role.

items.summary: MANCHESTER UNITED are replacing one Brazil midfielder with another. items.title: ‘I’ll step up’ – Jordan Pickford reveals shock ambition to do the unthinkable for England at the World Cup
items.summary: JORDAN PICKFORD is dreaming of being an England World Cup hero — by SCORING a penalty. items.title: Iconic World Cup final shirt worn by Pele in 1958 tipped to sell at auction for staggering sum
items.summary: THE shirt worn by Pelé in the 1958 World Cup final is set to sell for an estimated £4.5million. The Brazilian was 17 when he scored two goals against host nation Sweden in a 5-2 victory. He remains the youngest player to score in a World Cup final, and went on to win two more...

items.summary: ISTVAN KOVACS is used to brushing away nonsense from footballers - given he deals with rowdy pupils in his day job as a PE teacher. items.summary: KYLE WALKER is worried about three England World Cup squad decisions - and it could cost Thomas Tuchel. items.title: Iraola set to sign Liverpool contract on Thursday
items.summary: Andoni Iraola is expected to arrive on Merseyside on Thursday to sign his contract and become Liverpool's new head coach.

items.title: Konate reveals depression after deaths of Jota & father
items.summary: Liverpool defender Ibrahima Konate opens up on how the deaths of team-mate Diogo Jota and his father left him battling depression during his final season at the club.

items.summary: JOSE MOURINHO will be the new Real Madrid manager - but only on one condition. items.title: England to be powered by beans on toast at World Cup base as stars ditch posh food for home comforts in search for glory
items.summary: ENGLAND players will be tin it to win it at the World Cup — by eating beans on toast before games. It is one of the classic comfort food meals that will be on the menu to give the lads a rocket boost. Posh grub and fine wines have been banned from the team’s base...
